identity.name: Student Group
identity.definition: A Student Group is a named collection of students from a class, organized by skill level, assessment performance, or teacher selection, used to target instruction and differentiate assignments.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Student Group appears in the class grouping view

examples.name: Below Grade Level — Number Sense
examples.groupingCriteria: Star Math score below 40th percentile in Number Sense

examples.name: Geometry Review Group
examples.groupingCriteria: Freckle adaptive level below 3.5 in Geometry

stories.title: Create Differentiation Group
stories.benefit: I can target instruction to students with similar needs and assign differentiated work efficiently.
stories.whenClause: I create a student group based on assessment data or my own selection,
stories.thenClause: the group appears in my class view, I can assign work to the entire group at once, and I can track their collective progress.

stories.title: Accept System-Suggested Group
stories.benefit: I save time by leveraging AI-identified clusters of students with similar proficiency gaps.
stories.whenClause: I review a system-suggested student group from an Insight,
stories.thenClause: I can accept the suggestion to create the group, modify the membership, or dismiss the suggestion.

businessRules.title: Class Scoping
businessRules.description: A Student Group belongs to exactly one Class. All students in the group must be enrolled in that class.

businessRules.title: Creation Sources
businessRules.description: Groups can be created by teachers (manual selection) or by the system (from Insight-based suggestions). Source is tracked for analytics.

businessRules.title: Membership Flexibility
businessRules.description: Teachers can add or remove students from any group at any time, regardless of the original grouping criteria.

businessRules.title: Staleness Detection
businessRules.description: System-suggested groups become 'stale' when underlying assessment data has changed significantly since group creation. A refresh re-evaluates membership.

businessRules.title: No Overlapping Restriction
businessRules.description: Students can belong to multiple groups within the same class. Groups are not mutually exclusive.

businessRules.title: Assignment Targeting
businessRules.description: Groups serve as assignment targets. When a teacher assigns work to a group, all current members receive the assignment.

businessRules.title: Archival
businessRules.description: Archived groups are hidden from active views but retained for historical reporting. Archival does not affect existing assignments.

businessRules.title: Minimum Size
businessRules.description: A group must contain at least 1 student. Empty groups are automatically archived.

businessRules.title: Name Uniqueness
businessRules.description: Group names must be unique within a class. The system auto-generates names for system-suggested groups.

states.name: Active
states.description: Group is current and available for assignment targeting
states.triggers: Created by teacher or accepted from system suggestion

states.name: Stale
states.description: Underlying data has changed; membership may no longer reflect current proficiency
states.triggers: New assessment data significantly changes student proficiency relative to grouping criteria

states.name: Archived
states.description: Group is no longer active but data is retained
states.triggers: Teacher archives, or group becomes empty due to student transfers

relationships.mechanics: Students are added to groups by teacher selection or system suggestion. Teachers can freely modify membership at any time.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Group contains multiple Students; a Student can belong to multiple Groups within the same or different classes.
relationships.sorts: Students within a group: alphabetical by last name (default), by proficiency level in the associated skill.
relationships.filters: By proficiency level, by enrollment status.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is removed from the parent Class, they are automatically removed from all groups in that class.

relationships.mechanics: Each group is scoped to exactly one class. The group's student pool is limited to students enrolled in that class.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A Class can have multiple Groups; each Group belongs to one Class.
relationships.sorts: Groups within a class: by creation date, by student count, by status.
relationships.filters: By source (system-suggested vs. teacher-created), by status, by associated skill.
relationships.dependencies: If a Class is archived, all its groups are automatically archived.

relationships.mechanics: Groups serve as assignment targets. When work is assigned to a group, all current members receive the assignment.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Group can have multiple Assignments targeted to it; an Assignment can target multiple Groups.
relationships.sorts: Assignments for a group: by due date, by assignment type.
relationships.filters: By assignment type, by completion status.
relationships.dependencies: If a group is archived after an assignment is created, the assignment persists for existing members.

relationships.mechanics: Groups may be organized around a specific skill or metric. This association drives staleness detection and group report content.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A Group references at most one Skill; a Skill can be referenced by many Groups.
relationships.filters: By skill domain, by subject area.
relationships.dependencies: If the associated Skill is retired, the group continues to function but staleness detection may be affected.

relationships.mechanics: System-suggested groups originate from Insight recommendations. Accepting a grouping suggestion Insight creates the group.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-one (origin). A system-suggested Group originates from one Insight; teacher-created groups have no Insight origin.
relationships.filters: By whether the group originated from an Insight.
relationships.dependencies: If the originating Insight is dismissed after group creation, the group is unaffected.

relationships.mechanics: The teacher who created or accepted the group. Teachers can only manage groups in their own classes.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A Teacher can have many Groups; each Group has one owning Teacher.
relationships.dependencies: If the teacher is reassigned to a different class, group ownership may transfer.

relationships.mechanics: Group reports aggregate Scores for all students in the group. Used for tracking collective progress.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many (indirect through students). Group reports pull Scores for all member Students.
relationships.sorts: Scores within a group report: by date, by assessment type.
relationships.filters: By assessment type, by date range, by subject.
relationships.dependencies: Score availability depends on student assessment activity.

relationships.mechanics: Teachers may associate recommended resources with a group for targeted practice.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Group can have multiple recommended Resources; a Resource can be recommended to multiple Groups.
relationships.sorts: By relevance to the associated skill, by resource type.
relationships.dependencies: Resource recommendations are informational and do not create assignment obligations.

nestedObjects.name: Student
nestedObjects.description: Students included in this differentiation group

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: Aggregated scores for group members, used in group reports

allAttributes.name: Group Name
allAttributes.description: Display name of the student group. Auto-generated for system-suggested groups, user-defined for teacher-created groups.

allAttributes.name: Teacher
allAttributes.description: The teacher who owns and manages this group.

allAttributes.name: Class
allAttributes.description: The class this group is scoped to. All group members must be enrolled in this class.

allAttributes.name: Students
allAttributes.description: The students currently in this group.

allAttributes.name: Student Count
allAttributes.description: Derived count of students currently in the group.

allAttributes.name: Grouping Criteria
allAttributes.description: Description of how students were selected: assessment threshold, skill level, or teacher's rationale.

allAttributes.name: Source
allAttributes.description: Origin of the group: system-suggested (from Insight) or teacher-created (manual selection).

allAttributes.name: Associated Skill/Metric
allAttributes.description: The skill or metric the group is organized around. Optional for teacher-created groups.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Current state: active (usable), stale (data has changed), or archived (hidden from active views).

allAttributes.name: Last Refreshed
allAttributes.description: When group membership was last re-evaluated against current data. Applicable to system-suggested groups.

structure.evidence: A Student Group has rich attributes: name, teacher, class, student list, count, grouping criteria, source, associated skill, status, created date, and last refreshed. You can design a detail page for a Group.

instances.evidence: 'Below Grade Level — Number Sense' (6 students, system-suggested, 3rd Grade Math), 'Advanced Readers' (4 students, teacher-created, 5th Grade ELA), 'Geometry Review Group' (8 students, system-suggested, stale). Each class can have multiple Group instances.

purpose.evidence: Student Groups enable differentiated instruction. Teachers create or accept groups to target assignments, track progress by subgroup, and respond to AI-identified patterns. Groups bridge Insights and Assignments by providing a named target for instructional actions.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain container object. Named student subsets within a class that enable targeted instruction and differentiated assignment delivery.

synonyms.notes: Shortened form used in most UI surfaces
Technical term for system-identified student groupings
Implies a longer-lived grouping; Student Group is more fluid
Emphasizes the pedagogical purpose of the grouping

shapeshifterMatrix.description: Student groups within a class shown as cards with member count, criteria, and source. Teachers review existing groups, edit membership, or create new ones.
The complete group record — members, criteria, associated skill data, and all management controls. Teachers analyze group performance, refresh data-driven groups, or adjust membership.
Student groups listed as compact rows for selecting assignment targets. Teachers pick a group to assign work to instead of selecting individual students.
